title: carmichaelia uniflora var. suteri
intro: Carmichaelia uniflora var. suteri, also known as the Suter's broom, is a plant species native to New Zealand, belonging to the Fabaceae family. Traditionally, the plant's roots and leaves were used in Maori medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its reported antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Carmichaelia uniflora var. suteri are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant activity. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots are often used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are believed to alleviate symptoms associated with colds, flu, and digestive issues.
